channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: 2026–27 Federal Budget: what it means for early childhood education and care
item.description: The 2026–27 Federal Budget has been framed by Treasurer Jim Chalmers as one of the most important and ambitious budgets in decades, shaped by global uncertainty, cost-of-living pressure and the Government’s broader agenda of “resilience and reform”.
item.content:encoded: The 2026–27 Federal Budget has been framed by Treasurer Jim Chalmers as one of the most important and ambitious budgets in decades, shaped by global uncertainty, cost-of-living pressure and the Government’s broader agenda of “resilience and reform”.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Medium and small provider data collection begins for national ECEC pricing project
item.description: Data collection has commenced for medium and small early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers as part of the Australian Government’s Early Education Service Delivery Prices project.
item.content:encoded: Data collection has commenced for medium and small early childhood education and care (ECEC) providers as part of the Australian Government’s Early Education Service Delivery Prices project.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW families given clearer guidance on child safety in ECEC services
item.description: Families in New South Wales now have direct access to plain-language information about child safety in early childhood education and care (ECEC), with the NSW Early Learning Commission publishing resources that explain what a child safe service looks like, how concerns can be raised, and what parents and carers can expect from providers.
item.content:encoded: Families in New South Wales now have direct access to plain-language information about child safety in early childhood education and care (ECEC), with the NSW Early Learning Commission publishing resources that explain what a child safe service looks like, how concerns can be raised, and what parents and carers can expect from providers.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: National tool helps chart career pathways in early childhood education
item.description: Jobs and Skills Australia has launched an online tool designed to help Australians better understand how vocational education and training qualifications connect to employment opportunities, including within the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector.
item.content:encoded: Jobs and Skills Australia has launched an online tool designed to help Australians better understand how vocational education and training qualifications connect to employment opportunities, including within the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Rabobank and George the Farmer celebrate five years of connecting children to agriculture through books
item.description: Rabobank’s Rabo Community Fund has teamed up with the ‘edu-tainment’ brand ‘George the Farmer’, for its fifth year, to celebrate Children’s Book Week. Their collaboration is designed to teach children about where their food and fibre comes from in a fun and engaging way during Book Week, which this year takes place from August 22 to 28.
item.content:encoded: Rabobank’s Rabo Community Fund has teamed up with the ‘edu-tainment’ brand ‘George the Farmer’, for its fifth year, to celebrate Children’s Book Week. Their collaboration is designed to teach children about where their food and fibre comes from in a fun and engaging way during Book Week, which this year takes place from August 22 to 28.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.content:encoded: Prioritising intergenerational fairness must also extend to the wellbeing of our children - ABC Religion & Ethics - Australian Broadcasting Corporation
item.dc:creator: Australian Broadcasting Corporation

item.title: Kidi Kingdom fined $12,000 after child leaves service unnoticed for more than an hour
item.description: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Budget Falls Short for Aboriginal Children Despite Community-Led Solutions on the Table
item.description: Budget 2026 is one of missed opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and families.
item.content:encoded: Budget 2026 is one of missed opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and families.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Sector Peak Urges NT Government to Scrap Legislative Changes and Partner on Child Protection
item.description: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC is urging the NT Government to abandon proposed child protection legislative changes that would weaken the Child Placement Principle.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 8 May 2026
item.description: This week’s SNAICC in the News remembers Kumanjayi Little Baby and reflects on Sorry Business, community healing and Catherine Liddle’s calls for care.

item.title: Investigation needed into child protection leaks
item.description: SNAICC is calling for immediate investigation into child protection leaks of confidential information on Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family.

item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver
item.description: SNAICC has welcomed the release of the National Child and Family Investment Strategy as a landmark framework to shift investment to ACCOs.

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 17 April 2026
item.description: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.content:encoded: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
